[Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Summary

Weekly scheduled CI runs on commit `082f76e0f20f` (March 15 2026) recorded failures across 4 packages: `@azure/storage-blob`, `@azure/storage-file-datalake`, `@azure/search-documents`, and `@azure/communication-recipient-verification`. A total of **7 test failures** were found across `storage-blob` (3) and `storage-file-datalake` (4); the other two packages failed at the CI pipeline/infrastructure level with 0 actual test failures. All failures are in **`tests-weekly`** jobs (scheduled live-service tests) — regular, non-weekly CI runs on `main` are currently **green**.

---

## Failures

### `@azure/storage-blob`

**Check run:** `js - storage-blob - tests-weekly` (ID: 67145590433)
**Build summary:** Failed: 3 / 3,114 total (0.10 %, **3 new**)

**Failing tests:**
- `BlobServiceClient > createContainer and deleteContainer` in blob service live tests
- `ContainerClient > create with all parameters configured` in container client live tests
- `Highlevel > downloadToBuffer should success - without passing the buffer` in highlevel live tests

**Error:**
````
Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:0add0850-e01e-0030-64ae-b4f30b000000 Time:2026-03-15T19:00:53.4123436Z

Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:ebe9b61f-a01e-0101-53ae-b4ec15000000 Time:2026-03-15T19:00:38.8138261Z

The operation was aborted.  (Highlevel > downloadToBuffer)
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure Storage test account used by the weekly live-service run has had its **public access policy changed** (or the test subscription policy now blocks public blob/container access). Tests that call `createContainer` with `publicAccessType: 'blob'` or `publicAccessType: 'container'`, or that set an access policy via `ContainerClient.setAccessPolicy`, now receive an `AuthorizationFailure` / `PublicAccessNotPermitted` service error. The `downloadToBuffer` abort is likely a cascading timeout from a prior provisioning failure.

**Suggested fix:**
This is a **service/environment configuration** issue, not a code defect. Required actions:
1. Check whether the CI test storage account's "allow blob public access" setting was disabled (via Azure Policy or manual change).
2. Re-enable public access on the test storage account **or** update the test recordings/skip logic so tests requiring public access are skipped when public access is not enabled on the account.
3. For the `downloadToBuffer` abort, investigate whether a timeout increase or retry is warranted, or whether it is a cascade of the above.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live-service test; requires the CI test credentials and storage account)

---

### `@azure/storage-file-datalake`

**Check run:** `js - storage-file-datalake - tests-weekly` (ID: 67146779458)
**Build summary:** Failed: 4 / 1,750 total (0.23 %, **4 new**)

**Failing tests:**
- `DataLakeFileSystemClient > create with all parameters configured`
- `DataLakeFileSystemClient > createIfNotExists`
- `DataLakeFileSystemClient Node.js only > setAccessPolicy`
- `DataLakeFileSystemClient Node.js only > setAccessPolicy should work when expiry and start undefined`

**Error:**
```
Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:a69ba611-f01e-003a-6daf-b427b9000000 Time:2026-03-15T19:11:10.6842721Z

Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:f17b6bc3-001e-004c-4baf-b4adf1000000 Time:2026-03-15T19:14:08.9668075Z
```

**Root cause:**
Same root cause as `@azure/storage-blob` above. The tests that pass `publicAccess` options (e.g. `'blob'`, `'filesystem'`) or call `setAccessPolicy` fail because the underlying ADLS Gen2 / Blob Storage account now rejects public-access operations. All 4 failures are **new** (not recurring), confirming the storage account policy changed recently.

**Suggested fix:**
Same remediation as `@azure/storage-blob` — restore the "allow blob public access" setting on the CI test account, or conditionally skip / guard public-access test cases based on service capabilities.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live-service test)

---

### `@azure/search-documents`

**Check run:** `js - search-documents - tests-weekly` (ID: 67146748867)
**Build summary:** Failed: **0** / 510 tests — pipeline failed before/after tests ran

**Error:**
```
Not found workingDirectory: /Users/runner/work/1/s/$(PackagePath)
Error: Not found workingDirectory: /Users/runner/work/1/s/$(PackagePath)
    at Object._checkPath [as checkPath] (/Users/runner/work/_tasks/AzurePowerShell_…/5.269.2/…)
PowerShell exited with code '1'.
```

**Root cause:**
The CI pipeline step references a `$(PackagePath)` variable that is **not being expanded** (the literal string `$(PackagePath)` appears in the resolved working directory path). This is a CI pipeline YAML configuration issue — the variable is either undefined in this pipeline context or the variable group / template that defines it is not being included. All 510 tests that did run passed; the failure is purely infrastructure-level.

**Suggested fix:**
Investigate the weekly pipeline YAML for the `search-documents` package. Ensure the variable `PackagePath` is defined in the right variable group or template and is accessible at the step where it's used. This is a CI configuration fix, not a code change.

**Reproduced locally:** No (pipeline variable issue; not reproducible in local environment)

---

### `@azure/communication-recipient-verification`

**Check run:** `js - communication-recipient-verification - tests-weekly` (ID: 67144114961)
**Build summary:** 29 pipeline errors / 12 warnings — multiple sub-jobs failed

**Error (observed across all sub-jobs):**
```
Not found workingDirectory: /mnt/vss/_work/1/s/$(PackageTestPath)
Bash exited with code '127'.
PowerShell exited with code '1'.
````

**Root cause:**
Same class of CI pipeline issue as `search-documents` — the `$(PackageTestPath)` variable is not being expanded. The literal string `$(PackageTestPath)` is used as a working directory, causing all subsequent steps (install, build, test) to fail before any tests run. This affects all platform sub-jobs (Ubuntu, Windows, macOS).

**Suggested fix:**
Investigate the weekly pipeline YAML for `communication-recipient-verification`. Verify `PackageTestPath` is properly defined in the variable group or pipeline template. Compare against a passing package's pipeline definition to find the discrepancy.

**Reproduced locally:** No (pipeline configuration issue)

---

## Additional Notes

- **Regular CI on `main` is green.** The most recent fully-completed non-weekly CI run (commit `46ca18ce7be9`) shows 0 failures across 100 check runs.
- The **storage failures** (storage-blob and storage-file-datalake) share the same root cause: a recent Azure Storage account policy change disabling public access. These are **4 new** and **3 new** failures respectively, suggesting the policy change was recent (around March 15, 2026).
- The **pipeline variable failures** (search-documents and communication-recipient-verification) are infrastructure issues where `$(PackagePath)` / `$(PackageTestPath)` variables are undefined in the weekly pipeline context. These do not reflect any code-level regression — all 510 search-documents tests that ran passed.
- None of the failures are reproducible locally since they all require live Azure service credentials and/or the CI pipeline context.

[Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Summary

As of the HEAD commit on `main` ([a16db635](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]), 2026-03-06), CI is reporting **239 failing tests across 3 packages** plus numerous build/infrastructure failures. All test failures are in **live test runs** and are caused by external service instability (HTTP 500, AAD outage, and rate limiting) rather than code defects. Additionally, `maps-timezone-rest` has a CI variable substitution bug, `storage-blob` has a browser build failure, and 20+ `mgmt` packages have `PublishDocsToNightlyBranch` failures. None of the live test failures are marked as "new" except 3 in `openai` (also attributable to rate-limiting).

---

## Failures

### `@azure/communication-short-codes`

**Check runs:** `js - communication-short-codes - tests` (and 6 platform variants — all failed)  
**Build:** `#20260306.1` (buildId 5972619)  
**Test stats:** Failed 7 / 142 (4.93%; **0 new, 7 recurring**)

**Failing tests:**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments` in `sdk/communication/communication-short-codes/test/public/manageProgramBriefAttachment.spec.ts` (reported 4 times across platform matrix jobs)

**Error:**
````
The server encountered an internal error.
```

**Root cause:**
The test exercises a live Communication Services endpoint during a recorded/live test run. The remote server is returning an HTTP 500 "internal error" response. This is a recurring service-reliability issue (7 recurring, 0 new) unrelated to any code change on `main`.

**Suggested fix:**
No code fix required. The backend service needs to be investigated by the Azure Communication Services team. Consider adding retry logic or improving error resilience in the test if this is a known flaky endpoint.

**Reproduced locally:** Not applicable (live test only; no local service endpoint to reproduce against)

---

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Check runs:** `js - communication-identity - tests` (and 5 platform variants — all failed)  
**Build:** `#20260306.1` (buildId 5972430)  
**Test stats:** Failed 12 / 641 (1.87%; **0 new, 12 recurring**)

**Failing tests:**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live]` in `sdk/communication/communication-identity/test/public/node/getTokenForTeamsUser.node.spec.ts` (multiple variants: with and without AAD)

**Error:**
```
invalid_grant: Error(s): 5000224 - Timestamp: 2026-03-06 09:13:21Z -
Description: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: fb6901a2-b202-46b3-b905-3e6e84ff8800
Correlation ID: cb9be9b9-7421-4205-8e6d-71482c33da1c
```

**Root cause:**
Azure AD is returning `AADSTS5000224 – resource not available` for the Teams User token exchange call. This is a known Azure AD service degradation event. All 12 failures are **recurring**, confirming this is not caused by a recent code change.

**Suggested fix:**
No code fix required. This is an AAD service-availability issue. Monitor the [Azure Status page]((status.azure.com/redacted)) for the affected region. The test infrastructure team should investigate whether the test service principal or tenant subscription is still active.

**Reproduced locally:** Not applicable (live AAD service issue)

---

### `@azure/openai`

**Check runs:** `js - openai - tests` (and 4 platform variants — all failed)  
**Build:** `#20260306.1` (buildId 5972191)  
**Test stats:** Failed 220 / 2,166 (10.16%; **3 new, 217 recurring**)

**Failing tests (examples from annotations):**
- `Chat Completions [2025-04-01-preview] > chat.completions.create non-streaming > calls a specific tool if its name is specified > 'gpt-4o' ('2024-05-13')`
- `Chat Completions [2025-04-01-preview] > chat.completions.create stream > calls toolCalls > 'gpt-4o' ('2024-05-13')`
- `Legacy Completions [2025-04-01-preview] > completions.create > returns completions stream > 'gpt-4o' ('2024-05-13')`

**Error:**
```
429 Your requests to gpt-4o-ais-deployment-test for gpt-4o in East US have exceeded
the call rate limit for your current OpenAI S0 pricing tier. This request was for
ChatCompletions_Create under Azure OpenAI API version 2025-04-01-preview.
Please retry after 4 seconds. To increase your default rate limit, visit:
(aka.ms/redacted)
BaseURL: (sdk8428.openai.azure.com/redacted)
Deployment: gpt-4o-ais-deployment-test
```

**Root cause:**
The CI test deployment `gpt-4o-ais-deployment-test` (Azure OpenAI S0 tier in East US) is hitting its rate limit during the test run. The 217 recurring failures are a long-standing quota issue. The **3 new failures** are also attributable to the same rate-limiting pattern rather than a code change (no relevant code changes were detected in `sdk/openai/` for this period).

**Suggested fix:**
No immediate code fix required. The Azure OpenAI test infrastructure team should consider upgrading the deployment quota or adding exponential back-off with retry in the test harness. Upgrading from S0 to a higher tier (or increasing the TPM quota via (aka.ms/redacted) would resolve the recurring failures.

**Reproduced locally:** Not applicable (live service rate limiting)

---

### `@azure-rest/maps-timezone`

**Check runs:** `js - maps-timezone-rest - tests` (and 5 platform variants — all failed)  
**Build:** `#20260305.1` (buildId scheduled live test run)  
**Test stats:** Build failed before tests ran — **0 tests executed**

**Error (from CI annotations):**
```
Not found workingDirectory: /mnt/vss/_work/1/s/$(PackageTestPath)
Bash exited with code '127'.
````

**Root cause:**
The CI pipeline step `Convert tests to run against package references` (in `eng/pipelines/templates/jobs/live.tests.yml`, line 136) runs `eng/tools/dependency-testing/index.js`, which is responsible for emitting the `PackageTestPath` pipeline variable via `##vso[task.setvariable variable=PackageTestPath]...`. When this script fails or does not emit the variable, subsequent steps that reference `$(PackageTestPath)` receive the literal string `$(PackageTestPath)` as the working directory path, causing the "Not found workingDirectory" error.

The root cause is likely that the `dependency-testing` script failed for this package (e.g., could not resolve the pnpm workspace entry for `@azure-rest/maps-timezone`) and did not emit the variable. The test folder for this package is `test/public`, which **does** exist (`sdk/maps/maps-timezone-rest/test/public/mapsTimezone.spec.ts`), so the issue is in the dependency-testing script itself.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Add error-handling and diagnostics to `eng/tools/dependency-testing/index.js` to emit a clear failure message if the package cannot be found, rather than silently failing.
2. Consider adding a fallback in the pipeline step to fail fast with a descriptive error if `PackageTestPath` is empty after the script runs.

**Reproduced locally:** Not attempted (CI infrastructure issue, not a package code bug)

---

### `@azure/storage-blob` (browser tests)

**Check run:** `js - storage-blob - tests (Public windows_20x_browser)`  
**Build:** `#20260306.1` (buildId 5972558)  
**Test stats:** Build failed before tests ran — **0 tests recorded**

**Error:** `PowerShell exited with code '1'` (Build log #L644)

**Root cause:** A PowerShell setup step failed during the browser test configuration for storage-blob. The failure occurs in the CI setup stage, not in the package code itself. No annotations pointing to specific test failures are present.

**Suggested fix:** Investigate the PowerShell step at line 644 of the build log in Azure DevOps (buildId 5972558). This is likely a flaky infrastructure step.

**Reproduced locally:** Not attempted

---

## Additional Notes

### mgmt `PublishDocsToNightlyBranch` failures (20+ packages)

The following management-plane packages all have failures in the `Integration PublishDocsToNightlyBranch` step with `PowerShell exited with code '1'` and 0 test failures:

`appconfiguration`, `servicefabricmanagedclusters`, `azurestack`, `springappdiscovery`, `policy`, `storageactions`, `migrate`, `extendedlocation`, `orbital`, `cloudhealth`, `azureadexternalidentities`, `sphere`, `changeanalysis`, `resources`, `dnsresolver`, `impactreporting`, `edgeactions`, `appservice`, `devhub`, `cosmosforpostgresql`

All share the same failure pattern and appear to be caused by a systemic issue in the nightly docs-publishing pipeline step rather than individual package regressions. This may be related to the `.npmrc` authentication changes in [#37480](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/37480) (merged 2026-03-06).

### `documentintelligence` and `healthdataaiservices` build failures

Both packages show `Build failed` with `Script failed with exit code: 1` but **0 test failures** — all tests passed. The failure is in a non-test build step.

### Commit inspected

All check runs above are associated with HEAD commit [`a16db635`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (2026-03-06T07:39:34Z), a sync of `eng/common` from `azure-sdk-tools` PR 14361.

[Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Summary

CI checks on `main` at commit `[REDACTED]` (2026-03-23) show failures across **6 packages**: `@azure/communication-identity`, `@azure/storage-blob`, `@azure/communication-short-codes`, `@azure/openai`, `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence`, and `@azure/arm-changeanalysis`. The test failures (4 packages) are all caused by live-infrastructure issues (Azure service errors, rate limits, and storage account policy changes), while 2 packages have build/analyze pipeline failures with no test failures. Build #20260323.1 is the CI run inspected.

---

## Failures

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Failing tests:**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live]` (live test)
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD]` (live test)

**Error:**
````
invalid_grant: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 824d187e-7336-4f7c-884f-94c520ec0500
```

**Root cause:**
Azure Active Directory returned error code `5000224` indicating the target resource is temporarily unavailable on the Microsoft backend. This is a live-test-only failure affecting 12 tests (all recurring, 0 new). No code change triggered this failure.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. This is a service-side availability issue. The Azure Identity team should be notified if the failure persists. The affected tests should be retried once the service recovers.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure credentials and service availability)

---

### `@azure/storage-blob`

**Failing tests:**
- `BlobServiceClient > createContainer and deleteContainer`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as single block`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as separate blocks`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlock, stageBlockURL and commitBlockList with CPK`
- (14 additional related tests)

**Error:**
```
Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:84e2538d-501e-0057-22a3-bae3f7000000
Time:2026-03-23T08:57:34.1891632Z
````

**Root cause:**
The test storage account's public blob access policy was changed (or a new account was provisioned with public access disabled by default). The test `createContainer and deleteContainer` explicitly sets `access: "container"` to create a public container; the live test run against the current storage account rejects this with `PublicAccessNotPermitted`. All 18 failures are **new** (0 recurring), which strongly indicates a recent test-environment infrastructure change rather than a code regression.

The specific test code:
````typescript
// sdk/storage/storage-blob/test/blobserviceclient.spec.ts, line ~422
const access = "container";
const { containerClient } = await blobServiceClient.createContainer(containerName, {
  access,
  metadata,
});
```

**Suggested fix:**
No source code change is needed. The test environment should be updated to use a storage account that permits public access, or the tests should be reviewed to determine if they can be run against an account without public access enabled. Ticket should be filed with the EngSys/test-resources team to restore or replace the storage account configuration.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure storage account with public access enabled)

---

### `@azure/communication-short-codes`

**Failing tests:**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments`

**Error:**
```
The server encountered an internal error.
```

**Root cause:**
The Communication Short Codes service is returning HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors for the Attachments management API. This is a recurring failure (7 tests, 0 new) indicating an ongoing service-side issue, not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. The Azure Communication Services Short Codes team should be engaged to investigate the persistent server error in the Attachments API.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Communication Services endpoint)

---

### `@azure/openai`

**Failing tests:**
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.transcriptions.create > returns json transcription for mp3 files`
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.transcriptions.create > returns json transcription for mp4 files`
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.translations.create > returns json translation for mp3 files`
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.translations.create > returns json translation for mp4 files`
- (336 additional failures)

**Error:**
```
429 Your requests to whisper for whisper in Sweden Central have exceeded the call rate limit
for your current OpenAI S0 pricing tier. This request was for Audio_Transcriptions under
Azure OpenAI API version 2024-10-21. Please retry after 30 seconds.
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure OpenAI Whisper deployment in the Sweden Central region has hit its rate limit (S0 pricing tier TPM/RPM quota). 340 total tests failed (28 new), caused by the live test suite exhausting the quota. This is a test-infrastructure resource allocation issue, not a code bug.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change needed. Options include:
1. Increase the Whisper deployment quota/capacity in the Sweden Central test environment.
2. Add retry logic with back-off to the test utilities for 429 responses (already using `maxRetriesOption` but the retries may be exhausting within the test timeout).
3. Consider running Whisper tests in a separate region or pipeline with adequate quota.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure OpenAI endpoint with rate limits)

---

### `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence`

**Failing tests:** None (135 tests passed, 0 failed)

**Build failures:**
- `js - documentintelligence (Build Build)` — Script failed with exit code 1
- `js - documentintelligence (Build Analyze)` — PowerShell exited with exit code 1

**Error:**
```
Build log #L68: Script failed with exit code: 1
Build log #L63: PowerShell exited with code '1'.
```

**Root cause:**
The TypeScript build and/or API analysis steps are failing without surfacing specific error details in the GitHub check-run annotations. The detailed logs are in Azure DevOps (inaccessible from this environment). Possible causes include:
- The recent `[dev-tool] Add Package version field in api extractor output` change (commit `816e2a279`) may have altered the generated API review output, causing a diff against the checked-in `review/ai-document-intelligence-node.api.md` file.
- A broken link or README validation in the Analyze step.
- A TypeScript compilation error in `src/` or generated client code.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Check the full Azure DevOps build log for the `documentintelligence` pipeline run (Build #20260323.1).
2. Investigate whether the `extract-api` output changed after commit `816e2a279` by running `pnpm turbo build --filter=`@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence...` --token 1` locally and checking if `review/ai-document-intelligence-node.api.md` has uncommitted changes.
3. If the API review file changed, commit the updated file.

**Reproduced locally:** Not attempted — registry authentication required (`pkgs.dev.azure.com`)

---

### `@azure/arm-changeanalysis`

**Failing tests:** None (3 tests passed, 0 failed)

**Build failure:**
- `js - changeanalysis - mgmt (Build Analyze)` — Bash exited with exit code 1

**Error:**
```
Build log #L24: Bash exited with code '1'.
````

**Root cause:**
The Analyze pipeline step failed (spell check, link verification, README checks, or API validation). The package has no test failures, indicating this is a CI tooling or documentation issue. Without access to the Azure DevOps build log the exact step cannot be identified.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Check the full Azure DevOps build log for the `changeanalysis` pipeline run (Build #20260323.1).
2. Run the Analyze checks locally: verify links in `sdk/changeanalysis/`, run spell check, and validate the README.

**Reproduced locally:** Not attempted — registry authentication required

---

## Additional Notes

- **All 4 packages with unit test failures** (`communication-identity`, `storage-blob`, `communication-short-codes`, `openai`) are failing due to **live-test infrastructure issues** (Azure service errors, account policy changes, rate limits) — not code bugs. No source-code changes are needed for these.
- **The 2 packages with build failures** (`documentintelligence`, `changeanalysis`) have 0 test failures; the failure is in the CI pipeline Build/Analyze stage. These warrant investigation of the Azure DevOps logs.
- The recent `[dev-tool] Add Package version field in api extractor output` commit (`816e2a279`, 2026-03-19) may be a contributing factor to the `documentintelligence` build failure if the API review file needs to be regenerated.

[Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Summary

As of the HEAD commit [`952a2cc`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) on `main` (2026-03-17), CI reports **4 packages with failing check runs** (20 total failed check-run entries across all OS/Node matrix jobs). All failures were classified as **new** (0 recurring) by Azure Pipelines. The failures span live-test scenarios and are detailed below.

---

## Failures

### `@azure/digital-twins-core`

**Failing tests:**
- `DigitalTwins Relationships - create, read, list, delete operations > update relationship multiple` in `test/public/testRelationships.spec.ts`

**Check runs:** [`js - digital-twins-core - tests`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/23189405149) (2 runs, all platforms)  
**CI result:** Failed: 1 / Passed: 517 / Total: 525

**Error:**
````
RestError: There is no digital twin instance that exists with the ID DTRelationshipsTestsBuildingTwin.
Please verify that the twin id is valid and ensure that the twin is not deleted.
See: (aka.ms/redacted)

code: 'DigitalTwinNotFound'

Stack trace:
 ❯ handleErrorResponse  core/core-client/dist/esm/deserializationPolicy.js:148:17
 ❯ deserializeResponseBody  core/core-client/dist/esm/deserializationPolicy.js:79:43
```

**Root cause:**
The live test attempts to update a relationship on a digital twin named `DTRelationshipsTestsBuildingTwin`, but that twin does not exist in the test environment at the time the test runs. The test (`testRelationships.spec.ts`) normally creates the twin in a `beforeEach`/setup step; if a prior test run failed or was interrupted without cleanup, the service state may be inconsistent. This is a **live test environment issue**, not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
- Check whether a prior CI run left the Azure Digital Twins test environment in a partially-torn-down state (missing digital twins but models still present, or vice versa).
- Ensure `afterEach`/`afterAll` cleanup in `testRelationships.spec.ts` is robust to partial failures and always deletes twins regardless of test outcome.
- Consider adding a defensive pre-test cleanup step that removes any pre-existing twins/models with the test IDs before creating new ones.

**Reproduced locally:** No — live test requiring an Azure Digital Twins instance.

---

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Failing tests:**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD]` in `test/public/node/getTokenForTeamsUser.node.spec.ts`
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live]` in `test/public/node/getTokenForTeamsUser.node.spec.ts`

**Check runs:** [`js - communication-identity - tests`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/23189405149) (6 runs across all OS/Node matrix jobs)

**Error:**
```
ServerError: invalid_grant: Error(s): 5000224
Timestamp: 2026-03-17 09:11:23Z
Description: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available. If you are seeing
this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 681ce521-49bb-4d6b-a79a-ac4f779b0b00
Correlation ID: aaaf147c-1460-4f7f-ad43-49078ae5690e

Stack:
 ❯ ResponseHandler.validateToken  (msal-node internals)
```

**Root cause:**
Azure Active Directory is returning error code `AADSTS5000224` ("We are sorry, this resource is not available") when the test attempts to exchange a Teams user AAD token via MSAL. This error indicates that the AAD resource or scope required for Teams token exchange is currently unavailable — either a service outage, a test tenant misconfiguration, or a deprecation of the specific Microsoft 365 app/resource used by the test environment (`COMMUNICATION_M365_APP_ID`/`COMMUNICATION_M365_AAD_AUTHORITY`). This is **not a code regression**.

**Suggested fix:**
- Verify Azure AD / Microsoft 365 service health for the tenant used in CI (`COMMUNICATION_M365_AAD_TENANT`).
- Check whether the AAD application (`COMMUNICATION_M365_APP_ID`) used for the Teams token exchange has been modified, expired, or had permissions revoked.
- If this is a known service deprecation (Teams token exchange for Communication Services), consider skipping the affected test with `SKIP_INT_IDENTITY_EXCHANGE_TOKEN_TEST=true` and filing a tracking issue.

**Reproduced locally:** No — live test requiring AAD credentials and a Communication Services resource.

---

### `@azure/communication-short-codes`

**Failing tests:**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments` in `test/public/manageProgramBriefAttachment.spec.ts`

**Check runs:** [`js - communication-short-codes - tests`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/23189405149) (8 runs across all platforms)

**Error:**
```
RestError: The server encountered an internal error.
code: 'InternalError'
statusCode: 400
request URL: (livetesting.unitedstates.communication.azure.com/redacted)

Stack:
 ❯ handleErrorResponse  core/core-client/dist/esm/deserializationPolicy.js:148:17
 ❯ deserializeResponseBody  core/core-client/dist/esm/deserializationPolicy.js:79:43
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure Communication Services Short Codes live endpoint (`live-testing.unitedstates.communication.azure.com`) returns HTTP 400 with `InternalError` when the test attempts to create a US program brief attachment (`createOrReplaceUSProgramBriefAttachment`). This failure is consistent across all platforms and all CI matrix legs, suggesting a **server-side regression or configuration change** on the ACS live-testing endpoint rather than a client-side code bug.

**Suggested fix:**
- Report the 400 `InternalError` to the Azure Communication Services Short Codes service team and reference the failing request URLs in the annotations.
- Verify whether the live-testing endpoint has had a breaking API change or whether the test resource (program brief) is in an invalid state.
- If the endpoint issue cannot be resolved quickly, add a `skip` guard while the service-side issue is tracked.

**Reproduced locally:** No — live test requiring an Azure Communication Services resource.

---

### `arm-policy-profile-2020-09-01-hybrid` (`@azure/arm-policy-profile-2020-09-01-hybrid`)

**Failing tests:**
- `My test > sample test` in `test/policy.spec.ts`

**Check runs:** [`js - policy - mgmt`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/23189405149) (2 runs: Linux and macOS)  
**CI result:** Failed: 1 / Passed: 4 / Total: 23

**Error:**
```
RecorderError: Start request failed.
Serialized Error: { statusCode: undefined }

Stack:
 ❯ Recorder.start  test-utils/recorder/dist/esm/recorder.js:254:19
 ❯ test/policy.spec.ts:33:5 (beforeEach)
````

**Root cause:**
The `@azure-tools/test-recorder`'s `Recorder.start()` call in `beforeEach` fails because the test proxy is unreachable (`statusCode: undefined` = connection refused). The failing test itself (`policy.spec.ts`) is a trivial placeholder that only asserts `isTrue(true)`, meaning the test logic is not at fault. The root cause is that the **test proxy was not started** before the tests ran in this specific CI pipeline job. This is a **CI pipeline/infrastructure issue** affecting the `arm-policy-profile-2020-09-01-hybrid` package's mgmt test run.

Notably, the other `arm-policy` package (`arm-policy`) ran in the same pipeline and passed its tests, suggesting the test proxy startup step may have been skipped or failed specifically for the `arm-policy-profile-2020-09-01-hybrid` job.

**Suggested fix:**
- Investigate the CI YAML for the `policy` mgmt pipeline to ensure the test proxy is started before `arm-policy-profile-2020-09-01-hybrid` tests run.
- Check whether the test proxy startup is conditional on something that evaluates differently for this package.
- Re-run the failing check to determine whether this is a transient infrastructure flake.

**Reproduced locally:** No — requires the `azure-sdk-tools` test proxy to be running, and this is a CI infrastructure issue.

## Additional Notes

- All 4 failures were reported as **new** (0 recurring) by Azure Pipelines on this commit, which may indicate transient environment conditions rather than persistent code regressions.
- None of the failures are in `snippets.spec.ts` files (those were excluded per policy).
- None of the failures appear traceable to a specific code change in this commit or the recent commits on `main`. The most recent commits were: a loadtesting sourceType change, TypeSpec emitter dependency bumps, and eng/common syncs.
- Local reproduction of any of these failures is **not possible** without live Azure service credentials and a running test proxy, which are unavailable in this analysis environment.
- The `communication-identity` AADSTS5000224 error is particularly concerning — if the Microsoft 365 → Communication Services token exchange feature has been deprecated or the test AAD app has lapsed, the tests may need to be updated or the environment re-provisioned.

Seven packages have failing CI check runs on the `main` branch at commit [`734bf6b96b`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (2026-03-10T07:15:13Z). The most critical failure is `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence` (126 playback unit test failures) which has a clear, reproducible code bug. The remaining failures are live-service or CI-infrastructure issues.

---

## Failures

### `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence` (`sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest`)

**Failing tests (sample — all 126 playback tests fail with the same error):**
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > pdf file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > png file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > jpeg file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > tiff file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- (+ 122 more across all `*.spec.ts` node test files)

**Check run IDs:** `66422913994`, `66422788449`, `66421430703`, `66421185625`, `66421164425`

**Error:**
```
Unable to find a record for the request
  POST (endpoint/redacted)
Uri doesn't match:
  request ((endpoint/redacted)
  record  ((endpoint/redacted)
```

**Root cause:**
The playback `ENDPOINT` constant in `test/utils/constants.ts` is `"(endpoint/redacted) (trailing slash). The client factory in `src/documentIntelligence.ts` appends `/documentintelligence` to the endpoint parameter:

```typescript
// src/documentIntelligence.ts, line 28-29
const endpointUrl =
  options.endpoint ?? options.baseUrl ?? `\$\{endpointParam}/documentintelligence`;
```

`"(endpoint/redacted) + `"/documentintelligence"` → `"(endpoint/redacted) (double slash).

The test recordings (assets tag `js/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest_557bf0ed19`) store URLs with a **single** slash (`(endpoint/redacted), so every playback request mismatches every recording.

**Suggested fix (Option A — fix the test constant, minimal change):**

```diff
--- a/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/test/utils/constants.ts
+++ b/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/test/utils/constants.ts
@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@ export const EnvVarKeys = {
   TEST_MODE: "TEST_MODE",
 } as const;
 
-export const ENDPOINT = "(endpoint/redacted)
+export const ENDPOINT = "(endpoint/redacted)";
 export const KEY = "api_key";
 export const DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH = false;
```

**Suggested fix (Option B — defensive fix in source, also fixes live/record mode when Azure management API returns endpoints with trailing slashes):**

````diff
--- a/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/src/documentIntelligence.ts
+++ b/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/src/documentIntelligence.ts
@@ -26,7 +26,7 @@ export default function createClient(
 ): DocumentIntelligenceClient {
   const endpointUrl =
-    options.endpoint ?? options.baseUrl ?? `\$\{endpointParam}/documentintelligence`;
+    options.endpoint ?? options.baseUrl ?? `\$\{endpointParam.replace(/\/$/, "")}/documentintelligence`;
```

Option B is preferred as it is more defensive and handles the case where the Azure management plane (`CognitiveServicesManagementClient.accounts.get(…).properties.endpoint`) returns an endpoint with a trailing slash in live/record mode.

**Note:** `sdk/formrecognizer/ai-form-recognizer/test/utils/constants.ts` has the same `"(endpoint/redacted) constant; its client factory should be audited for the same trailing-slash concatenation.

**Reproduced locally:** Unable to reproduce (pnpm install fails due to Azure DevOps feed authentication requirements in CI), but the root cause is unambiguous from reading the source code and recordings.

### `@azure/attestation` (`sdk/attestation/attestation`)

**Failing test:**
- `AttestationClient in Browser > #attestTpm` in `test/browser/attestationTests.browser.spec.ts`

**Check run IDs:** `66434853540`, `66434806065`

**Error:**
```
A VBS attestation policy has not been set on the attestation provider.
```

**Root cause:**
This is a **live service test** failure. The test attempts to perform TPM attestation, which requires a VBS (Virtualization Based Security) policy to be pre-configured on the test attestation provider. The CI test environment's provider either lacks this policy or the policy was reset. This is a service/environment configuration issue, not a code bug.

**Suggested fix:** Re-configure the VBS attestation policy on the attestation provider used by CI. No code change required.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Attestation service)

CI check runs on commit [`6687e2f`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (HEAD of `main` as of 2026-03-11) show failures across 4 packages and 3 build pipelines. The most actionable failure is **1 new** live-test failure in `@azure/storage-blob` from the recently merged CRC64 checksum feature (PR #37208), surfacing a potential bug in the structured message encoding. The remaining failures are either recurring live-test issues caused by external Azure service outages, or CI pipeline infrastructure errors (HTTP 503 from Azure Pipelines agent).

---

## Failures

### `@azure/storage-blob` — `js - storage-blob - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66596167217 (aggregate), 66594829356 (ubuntu node)

**Test counts:** 10 failed (1 new, 9 recurring) / 2,795 passed / 309 other / 3,114 total

**Failing tests (new):**
- `ContentChecksumValidation with client config - CRC64 > uploadStream should update progress event` in `sdk/storage/storage-blob/test/node/contentchecksum.spec.ts` (added in #37208)

**Error:**
```
The Structured Message was not valid.
RequestId:892e6d92-901e-0005-3235-b19f1f000000
Time:2026-03-11T08:58:50.6698569Z
```

**Root cause:**
This test runs only in live mode and was added as part of PR #37208 (CRC64 structured-message checksum for Storage). The Azure Storage service is rejecting the structured message with "not valid". Investigation of `sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts` reveals two suspicious code issues:

1. **`fillInt64` copies only 6 bytes instead of 8 (Node.js path)**  
   Line 186: `for (let index = 0; index < 6; ++index)` — the loop should iterate 8 times to write a full 64-bit integer. Currently it leaves bytes 6–7 of the target buffer at their zero-initialized value, which is harmless for sizes < 2^48 but is still a latent bug.

2. **Message header stores `messageLength` (total, with framing) where the comment says "content length"**  
   Line 108:
   ```ts
   this.fillInt64(this.messageHeaderBuffer, 1, this.messageLength); // content length
   ```
   `this.messageLength` is computed as:
   ```ts
   this.messageLength =
     this.contentLength +
     MESSAGE_HEADER_LENGTH +
     (SEGMENT_HEADER_LENGTH + FOOTER_LENGTH) * this.segmentsCount +
     FOOTER_LENGTH;
   ```
   This is the **total framed message length** (for a 4 MB block: `4,194,304 + 39 = 4,194,343`), whereas the comment, and likely the service's expectation, is for the **original unframed content length** (`4,194,304`). The service also receives `x-ms-structured-content-length: (contentLength)` as a header, so a mismatch between that and the value embedded in the binary message header could explain the rejection.

**Suggested fix:**

For the `fillInt64` loop length:

```diff
--- a/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
+++ b/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
@@ -183,7 +183,7 @@
       const internalBuffer = Buffer.alloc(8);
       internalBuffer.writeBigUInt64LE(BigInt(input));

-      for (let index = 0; index < 6; ++index) {
+      for (let index = 0; index < 8; ++index) {
         buffer[offset + index] = internalBuffer[index];
       }
```

For the message-header content-length field, if the spec requires the unframed content length:

````diff
--- a/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
+++ b/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
@@ -106,7 +106,7 @@
   private handlingMessageHeader() {
     this.messageHeaderBuffer[0] = MESSAGE_VERSION;

-    this.fillInt64(this.messageHeaderBuffer, 1, this.messageLength); // content length
+    this.fillInt64(this.messageHeaderBuffer, 1, this.contentLength); // content length
```

> **Note:** Before applying the second fix, the structured message format spec should be confirmed (e.g. with the Azure Storage team) to verify whether offset 1–8 of the stream header is supposed to carry the original content length or the full encoded message length.

**Reproduced locally:** Not attempted — test requires `TEST_MODE=live` with live Azure Storage credentials.

### `@azure/digital-twins-core` — `js - digital-twins-core - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66599073930 (aggregate), 66598833779 (browser)

**Test counts:** 1 failed (1 new) / 517 passed / 7 other / 525 total

**Failing test:**
- `DigitalTwins - create, read, update, delete and telemetry operations > query digital twin` in `sdk/digitaltwins/digital-twins-core/test/` (live test)

**Error:**
```
Twin DTRelationshipsTestsFloorTwin has active relationships.
Cannot delete a twin unless all the relationships are deleted.
Please ensure that the active relationships on the twin are deleted.
See section on retrieving relationships (aka.ms/redacted)
```

**Root cause:**
This is a live-test environment issue. A previous test run left behind a twin (`DTRelationshipsTestsFloorTwin`) with active relationships in the Azure Digital Twins live instance, preventing the current run's cleanup/setup from deleting it. This is an orphaned-resource problem, not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change needed. The live test environment should be cleaned up (delete orphaned twins/relationships). The test framework could also be hardened to tolerate pre-existing relationships by explicitly deleting relationships before deleting twins in `beforeEach`/`afterEach` hooks.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Digital Twins service).

**Check-run IDs:** 66596918581 (aggregate), 66596909408, 66596782728, 66596635512, 66596235247, 66596143649, 66596034022

invalid_grant: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 03a42967-b7ea-4d8b-b595-d1b3e8b80900

**Root cause:**
Azure Active Directory is returning `AADSTS5000224` (resource unavailable) for the Teams-user token grant. All 12 failures are **recurring** (pre-existing) and cross all test platforms (macOS, Windows, Ubuntu). This is a service-side AAD issue with the live test credential / tenant configuration — not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. The CI live-test credential for Teams user tokens should be refreshed or the service issue should be investigated with the Azure AD team.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live AAD credentials for the specific tenant).

### `@azure/communication-short-codes` — `js - communication-short-codes - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66594813221 (aggregate), and 6 platform-specific runs

The Azure Communication Services short-codes backend is returning HTTP 500 for attachment management operations. All 7 failures are **recurring**, appearing across all platforms, indicating a persistent service-side issue rather than a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. The Azure Communication Services short-codes service health should be investigated, or the live test environment credentials / subscription should be re-provisioned.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Communication Services credentials).

## Additional Notes

- The **storage-blob** failure is the only **new** test regression this cycle and is directly linked to the recently merged CRC64 structured-message checksum feature (PR #37208 landed 2026-03-06).
- All **communication-identity** and **communication-short-codes** failures are long-running recurring issues unrelated to recent code changes.
- The build-step failures (`js - core`, `js - dataprotection - mgmt`, `js - appconfiguration - mgmt`) do not affect test results and are likely transient CI infrastructure issues.
- Fixes for the `StructuredMessageEncoding.ts` issues should be paired with a new unit test (offline, no live service required) that validates the byte layout of an encoded structured message header against a known reference buffer.

test failures on main: `@azure/event-hubs`","body":"## Summary

CI analysis of commit [`50208323`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (2026-04-20) found **1 package** with actual unit test failures on the CI (playback) pipeline: **`@azure/event-hubs`**, with **9 failing tests** (all recurring). All other failing check runs were caused by infrastructure issues (OIDC token timeouts, missing artifact paths, browser runner exits, broken external links) with **0 test failures** in their test results.

The known failures tracking issue #37864 lists no known pre-existing failures, so these recurring failures are undocumented.

## Failures

### `@azure/event-hubs`

**Check run:** `js - eventhub (Build UnitTest ubuntu_22x_node)`  
**Test results:** 9 failed / 1,288 passed / 1,387 total (0 new, 9 recurring)

**Failing tests** in `sdk/eventhub/event-hubs/test/internal/client.spec.ts`:
- `EventHubClient > EventHubConsumerClient with non existent namespace > should throw ServiceCommunicationError for getEventHubProperties`
- `EventHubClient > EventHubConsumerClient with non existent namespace > should throw ServiceCommunicationError for getPartitionProperties`
- `EventHubClient > EventHubConsumerClient with non existent namespace > should throw ServiceCommunicationError for getPartitionIds`
- `EventHubClient > EventHubConsumerClient with non existent namespace > should throw ServiceCommunicationError while subscribe()`
- (and 5 more from the `EventHubProducerClient with non existent namespace` suite)

**Error (from CI annotations):**
```
expected AggregateError { errors: [ ...(4) ] } to be an instance of MessagingError
```

**Root cause:**

The tests use a fake connection string (`Endpoint=sb://a;...`) to verify that connecting to a non-existent namespace throws a `MessagingError`. The test helper `validateConnectionError` in the test file asserts:

```typescript
expect(err)
  .to.be.an.instanceOf(MessagingError)
  .and.has.property("code", isNodeLike ? "ENOTFOUND" : "ServiceCommunicationError");
```

In **Node.js 22+**, DNS resolution failures are reported as an `AggregateError` (aggregating IPv4 + IPv6 lookup failures) rather than a plain system error with `code: "ENOTFOUND"`. The `translate()` function in `sdk/core/core-amqp/src/errors.ts` does **not** handle `AggregateError` — it falls through to the last branch (`return isError(errObj) ? errObj : ...`) and returns the raw `AggregateError` untranslated. Because the raw `AggregateError` has no `.retryable` property, the retry loop breaks after one attempt and throws the single-element `errors[0]` = the raw `AggregateError` directly, bypassing the expected `MessagingError` wrapping.

**Suggested fix:**

Add `AggregateError` handling to `translate()` in `sdk/core/core-amqp/src/errors.ts`, immediately after the `TypeError`/`RangeError` guard:

```diff
--- a/sdk/core/core-amqp/src/errors.ts
+++ b/sdk/core/core-amqp/src/errors.ts
@@ -682,6 +682,12 @@ export function translate(err: unknown): MessagingError | Error {
   if (errObj instanceof TypeError || errObj instanceof RangeError) {
     return errObj;
   }
 
+  // Node.js 22+ reports DNS resolution failures as an AggregateError (multiple queries fail).
+  // Translate by recursively processing the first inner error, which carries the ENOTFOUND details.
+  if (errObj instanceof AggregateError && errObj.errors.length > 0) {
+    return translate(errObj.errors[0]);
+  }
+
   if (isAmqpError(errObj)) {
```

With this fix, when Node.js throws `AggregateError { errors: [{ code: "ENOTFOUND", syscall: "getaddrinfo", ... }] }`, `translate()` recurses on the first inner error, which is a system error recognized by `isSystemError()`. The result is a `MessagingError` with `code: "ENOTFOUND"` and `retryable: false`, exactly what the tests expect.

**Reproduced locally:** No — `pnpm install` could not complete in this environment because the workspace `.npmrc` points to a private Azure DevOps registry that requires authentication. The root cause was determined by static code analysis.

---

## Additional Notes

### Infrastructure-only failures (no test failures)

The following CI check runs also failed on the same commit but had **0 test failures** — their failures were purely infrastructural:

| Pattern | Affected packages | Infra error |
|---|---|---|
| `Build Build` — manifest generation | `batch`, `batch-mgmt`, `cognitiveservices-mgmt`, `links-mgmt`, `resources-mgmt` | `Path does not exist: /mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish` |
| `Build UnitTest *_browser` — browser runner exit | `relationships-mgmt`, `tenantactivitylogalerts-mgmt`, `servicegroups-mgmt`, `previewalertrule-mgmt` | `Cmd.exe exited with code '255'` |
| `Build UnitTest macoslatest*` — test-proxy | `oracledatabase-mgmt`, `contentunderstanding`, `onlineexperimentation` | `Path does not exist: test-proxy.log` / OIDC token timeout |
| `Integration PublishDocsToNightlyBranch` | `containerregistry`, `storage`, `textanalytics`, `tables` | `PowerShell exited with code '1'` |
| `Aggregate Reports GenerateReports` | aggregate-reports | Broken external link `(tsx.is/redacted) + PoliCheck errors |

These are not code regressions and are not included in this issue.

failures on main: broken pnpm-lock.yaml causes widespread pnpm install failures","body":"## Summary

A broken `pnpm-lock.yaml` introduced by the automatic pnpm-update commit ([a6df5fe](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) on Apr 13 2026) is causing every CI pipeline on `main` to fail. The lockfile importer section for `sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth` references `vitest@4.1.2` and `@vitest/coverage-istanbul@4.1.2`, but the snapshots section only contains entries for `4.1.4`. All 421 other packages were correctly updated to `4.1.4`. Since every CI pipeline runs `pnpm install` against the shared lockfile, every pipeline fails at the install step.

Inspected CI runs on commit `[REDACTED]` (Apr 14 2026 — the most recent commit with completed CI). **186 CI pipeline jobs** (Build UnitTest + Build Analyze) are failing across 20+ service directories including `notificationhubs`, `search`, `iot`, `template`, `fluidrelay-mgmt`, `applicationinsights-mgmt`, `eventhub-mgmt`, `datafactory-mgmt`, and many more.

## Failures

### All packages (systemic — `pnpm install` fails for the whole workspace)

**Failing tests:** N/A — CI pipelines fail before any tests run.

**Error (from CI annotations):**
```
ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_MISSING_DEPENDENCY  Broken lockfile: no entry for
'`@vitest/coverage-istanbul`@4.1.2(vitest@4.1.2)' in pnpm-lock.yaml

This issue is probably caused by a badly resolved merge conflict.
To fix the lockfile, run 'pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile'.
```

Confirmed locally — running `pnpm install` at the repo root fails immediately with this error.

**Root cause:**

The `pnpm update` PR ([a6df5fe](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED])) bumped `vitest`/`@vitest/coverage-istanbul` from `4.1.2` → `4.1.4` for all 421 workspace packages and updated the snapshots section accordingly (removing `4.1.2` snapshot entries and adding `4.1.4`). However, it failed to update the importer section for `sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth`, which still references:

- `@vitest/coverage-istanbul`: `version: 4.1.2(vitest@4.1.2)` (no such snapshot exists)
- `vitest`: `version: 4.1.2(`@opentelemetry/api`@1.9.1)(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(`@vitest/browser-playwright`@4.1.2)(vite@7.3.1(...))` (no such snapshot exists)

Because `pnpm install` runs for the entire workspace in every CI pipeline (no per-package filter), this single stale lockfile entry breaks every pipeline.

**Suggested fix:**

```diff
--- a/pnpm-lock.yaml
+++ b/pnpm-lock.yaml
@@ sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth devDependencies @@
       '`@vitest/coverage-istanbul`':
         specifier: catalog:testing
-        version: 4.1.2(vitest@4.1.2)
+        version: 4.1.4(vitest@4.1.4)
 ...
       vitest:
         specifier: catalog:testing
-        version: 4.1.2(`@opentelemetry/api`@1.9.1)(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(`@vitest/browser-playwright`@4.1.2)(vite@7.3.1(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(terser@5.46.1)(tsx@4.21.0)(yaml@2.8.3))
+        version: 4.1.4(`@opentelemetry/api`@1.9.1)(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(`@vitest/browser-playwright`@4.1.4)(`@vitest/coverage-istanbul`@4.1.4)(vite@7.3.1(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(terser@5.46.1)(tsx@4.21.0)(yaml@2.8.3))
```

The `4.1.4` snapshot entries already exist at lines 41609 and 45097 of the current lockfile, so no new snapshots need to be added.

Alternatively, regenerate the lockfile by running `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile` at the repo root (which requires the private Azure DevOps registry to be accessible).

**Reproduced locally:** Yes — `pnpm install` fails with `ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_MISSING_DEPENDENCY` on the current `main`.

---

## Additional Notes

- The "After APIView" check runs that appear as failures on the `b9e6755` commit are unrelated — those are GitHub Actions failures for API review on PRs from forked repos and do not represent test failures.
- The CI test-proxy log errors (`Path does not exist: .../test-proxy.log`) are a downstream symptom of the `pnpm install` failure — pipelines fail before the test-proxy is started.
- The broken entry is in the `sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth` importer section at lines ~23503–23526 of `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

test failures on main: attestation, communication-identity, communication-short-codes, digital-twins-core","body":"## Summary

As of 2026-03-26, commit [`7066e51`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (HEAD of `main`), CI reports failures across 4 service directories with actual test failures and 2 additional directories with CI-infrastructure-level failures (0 failed tests). All test failures occur exclusively in **live/AAD test mode** — none are reproducible locally without Azure credentials. The communication-identity and communication-short-codes failures are marked **recurring** (pre-existing), while the attestation failures are **new** (22 new failures).

---

## Failures

### `@azure/attestation`

**Failing tests (22 new failures, check run [68686295192](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68686295192)):**
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestOpenEnclaveShared`
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestOpenEnclaveAad`
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestOpenEnclaveIsolated`
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestSgxEnclaveShared`
- *(and ~18 additional AAD-mode attestation tests)*

**Stats:** 22 failed / 190 passed / 212 total (10.38% failure rate)

**Error:**
```
AttestationToken is not yet valid.
```

**Root cause:**
The live attestation service returns JWT tokens with an `nbf` (not-before) claim that is more than 10 seconds ahead of the CI runner's clock. In `sdk/attestation/attestation/test/utils/recordedClient.ts` the client is configured with:
```ts
validateNotBeforeTime: !isPlaybackMode(),
timeValidationSlack: 10, // 10 seconds slack in validation time.
```
And in `src/models/attestationToken.ts` the check is:
```ts
if (nbfTime > timeNow) {
  const delta = nbfTime - timeNow;
  if (delta > (options.timeValidationSlack ?? 0)) {
    problems.push("AttestationToken is not yet valid.");
  }
}
```
The 10-second `timeValidationSlack` is no longer sufficient — the clock skew between CI runners and the attestation service now exceeds this threshold.

**Suggested fix:**
Increase `timeValidationSlack` in `recordedClient.ts` to a larger value (e.g., 30–60 seconds), or widen the slack only when running in CI:

````diff
--- a/sdk/attestation/attestation/test/utils/recordedClient.ts
+++ b/sdk/attestation/attestation/test/utils/recordedClient.ts
@@ ... @@
         validateNotBeforeTime: !isPlaybackMode(),
-        timeValidationSlack: 10, // 10 seconds slack in validation time.
+        timeValidationSlack: 60, // 60 seconds slack to handle CI clock skew.
```
(The same change appears in two places in `recordedClient.ts`, at approximately lines 78–80 and 112–113.)

**Reproduced locally:** No (live AAD test, requires Azure credentials)

---

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Failing tests (12 recurring failures, check run [68682302045](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68682302045)):**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] > *` (multiple parameterized variants)
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD] > *` (multiple parameterized variants)

**Test file:** `sdk/communication/communication-identity/test/public/node/getTokenForTeamsUser.node.spec.ts`

**Stats:** 12 failed / 509 passed / 641 total (1.87% failure rate, all recurring)

**Error:**
```
invalid_grant: Error(s): 5000224 - Timestamp: 2026-03-26 09:14:47Z
Description: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 97f8975a-d085-407d-aa3d-8dff81751700
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure AD tenant/resource used for `Get Token For Teams User` live tests returns `AADSTS5000224`, indicating the targeted resource (Microsoft Teams user AAD resource in the test tenant) is unavailable or decommissioned. These tests are marked **recurring** — they have been failing consistently across multiple CI runs. This is a live service/tenant configuration issue, not a code bug.

**Suggested fix:**
This is an infrastructure issue. The test tenant needs to have the Teams user resource properly provisioned, or the live test environment needs to be refreshed. As a short-term workaround, the tests could be skipped via the existing `SKIP_INT_IDENTITY_EXCHANGE_TOKEN_TEST=true` environment variable until the tenant is fixed.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live AAD test, requires Azure credentials and tenant configuration)

---

### `@azure/communication-short-codes`

**Failing tests (7 recurring failures, check run [68680458910](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68680458910)):**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments` (multiple platform variants)

**Stats:** 7 failed / 134 passed / 142 total (4.93% failure rate, all recurring)

**Error:**
```
The server encountered an internal error.
```

**Root cause:**
The Short Codes service is returning HTTP 500 internal server errors for the `manage Attachments` live test scenario. These are **recurring** failures — a persistent service-side issue, not a code bug. The live test environment may have stale or invalid attachment resources.

**Suggested fix:**
This is a service-side infrastructure issue. The live test resources (short code attachments) may need to be refreshed or the test environment re-provisioned. No code change is needed.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live service test, requires Azure resources)

---

### `@azure/digital-twins-core`

**Failing tests:** 0 (518 passed / 7 skipped / 525 total)

**Check run:** [68683989356](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68683989356) — marked as failed despite 0 test failures.

**Error:**
```
Error while trying to get OIDC token: Error: Request timeout: /azure-sdk/_apis/distributedtask
Error while trying to get OIDC token: Error: connect ETIMEDOUT 150.171.73.16:443
````

**Root cause:**
The CI pipeline failed to acquire an OIDC token from Azure DevOps due to a network timeout. This is a transient CI infrastructure issue unrelated to any code change. All 518 tests actually passed.

**Suggested fix:**
Re-run the CI pipeline. No code change is needed.

**Reproduced locally:** No (CI infrastructure issue)

---

## CI Infrastructure Failures (0 test failures)

| `@azure/core-*` (core) | [68672400574](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68672400574) | 6,908 passed, 0 failed, 73 skipped | `Cmd.exe exited with code '1'` in CI browser test orchestration (also Python traceback in `Build Analyze` sub-job) |

These are CI pipeline configuration or runner issues, not test failures. No code change is needed.

---

## Additional Notes

- **attestation** is the only package with **new** failures (22 new); all others with test failures are **recurring**.
- All test failures occur exclusively in **live/AAD mode**. Playback-mode tests are unaffected.
- The attestation `timeValidationSlack` fix is the only code change that could directly address one of these failures.
- Local reproduction was not possible in this environment due to the private Azure DevOps npm registry (`pkgs.dev.azure.com`) requiring authentication that is not available in the CI analysis environment.
- CI run date: 2026-03-26, Build `#20260326.1`.

{"number":38090,"title":"CI test failures on main: `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration`","body":"## Summary

1 package affected, 1 test failing as of CI build `#20260410.1` (commit [`41b10c38`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]), 2026-04-10). The test recorder fails to start a playback session with the test proxy, causing the `operation list test` in `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration` to fail. This is a **new** failure (1 new, 0 recurring) that first appeared on the same commit that added `arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions` to the `js - kubernetesconfiguration - mgmt` CI pipeline (PR #38018).

## Failures

### `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration`

**Failing tests:**
- `kubernetesconfiguration test > operation list test` in `sdk/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration/test/kubernetesconfiguration_operations_test.spec.ts`

**Check run:** `js - kubernetesconfiguration - mgmt (Build UnitTest macoslatest_24x_node)` → [run 70757322962](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/70757322962)

**CI test summary:** Failed: 1 (1 new, 0 recurring) / Passed: 6 / Other: 12 / Total: 19

**Error:**
```
RecorderError: Start request failed.
 ❯ Recorder.start ../../test-utils/recorder/src/recorder.ts:336:19
 ❯ test/kubernetesconfiguration_operations_test.spec.ts:41:5

Serialized Error: { statusCode: undefined }
```

**Root cause:**

The `Recorder.start()` call in `beforeEach` POSTs to the test proxy to begin a playback session. The test proxy responded with a non-200 HTTP status (the status code is not propagated into the `RecorderError` constructor, so it surfaces as `statusCode: undefined`). This is a **test-infrastructure failure**, not a code or test-logic bug:

- The recording file **exists** in `Azure/azure-sdk-assets` at tag `js/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration_615562201f`, path `js/sdk/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration/recordings/node/kubernetesconfiguration_test/recording_operation_list_test.json`.
- The recording format and content are valid (single `GET /providers/Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration/operations?api-version=2023-05-01` → 200 response with a `value` array of 27 operations).
- 6 other tests passed in the same CI run, confirming the test proxy **was** running for part of the pipeline.
- The failure is classified as **1 new, 0 recurring**, indicating it appeared for the first time on this specific CI run.
- The triggering commit (`41b10c38` / PR #38018) added `arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions` to `ci.mgmt.yml` for the first time, expanding the set of packages tested in a single CI run from 4 to 5.

The most likely explanation is a test proxy crash or resource-contention issue on the macOS Node 24 CI runner when managing asset-sync checkout across the newly expanded set of 5 packages. The test proxy must switch between asset tags (`arm-kubernetesconfiguration_615562201f` and the newly added `arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions_e78462a13b`) during the run, which may have caused a state issue.

**Suggested fix:**

No code change is required — the recording and source are correct. The recommended investigation steps are:

1. **Re-trigger the CI pipeline** on `main` to determine whether this is a transient failure. If it passes on a re-run, add the test to a flakiness watchlist.
2. **Check test proxy asset-sync behavior** when multiple packages share a service directory and have different asset tags. Specifically, verify the test proxy correctly isolates the working directory for each package's `assets.json` tag when running sequentially.
3. If the failure recurs, **check macOS Node 24 test proxy logs** in Azure DevOps build `#20260410.1` for the `Build UnitTest macoslatest_24x_node` job to see the exact HTTP status code returned by the test proxy to `recorder.start()`.

**Reproduced locally:** No (test proxy binary download fails in sandboxed network environment; the recording and source code are structurally correct)

---

## Additional Notes

**`js - openai (Build Build)` failure excluded:** The openai CI pipeline explicitly sets `RunUnitTests: false` (see `sdk/openai/ci.yml`). Its "Build Build" failure (`Manifest generation failed` / `Path does not exist: /mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish`) is a CI packaging/dev-publish infrastructure failure, not a unit test failure, and is therefore out of scope for this issue.




> Generated by [Analyze CI Test Failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/24238441251/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.3M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-js+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-js%2Ffix-test-failures%22&type=issues)

## Release Preparation

Sets release date to **2026-04-22** for both packages and finalizes
changelogs.

### @azure/core-amqp 4.4.2
- **Bug fix**: Handle `AggregateError` in `translate()` for Node.js 20+
Happy Eyeballs (#38233)
- **Bug fix**: Clamp negative `setTimeout` values to 0 for Node.js v24+
(#38166)

### @azure/event-hubs 6.0.4
- **Bug fix**: Clamp negative timeout computations to 0 for Node.js v24+
(#38166)
- **Perf**: Event-driven queue signals, simplified serialization,
optimized cloning (#37882)

## Changes

### New test files
- base64.spec.ts (node + browser)
- interfaceHelpers.spec.ts
- operationHelpers.spec.ts
- pipeline.spec.ts
- utils.spec.ts
- urlHelpers.spec.ts (expanded)

### Expanded existing tests
- deserializationPolicy.spec.ts: error paths, XML parsing, header
deserialization
- serializationPolicy.spec.ts: body serialization edge cases, stream
bodies
- serializer.spec.ts: polymorphism, flattening, readonly, date/bytearray
edge cases
- serviceClient.spec.ts: auth, custom pipelines, error handling, base
URI
- authorizeRequestOnClaimChallenge.spec.ts: challenge parsing edge cases
- authorizeRequestOnTenantChallenge.spec.ts: multi-call token flows

### Test quality improvements
- Replaced mutable request capture with vi.fn() pattern
- Replaced try/catch + assert.fail with rejects.toThrow() /
toMatchObject()
- Replaced calledOnce flags with vi.fn().mockImplementationOnce()
- deepEqual on primitives changed to strictEqual
- assert.equal(x, undefined) changed to assert.isUndefined(x)

---------

- Spec API version: 2026-04-01-preview","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-22T18:26:02Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-22T18:26:02Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Fix TimeoutNegativeWarning in event-hubs CBS auth (#38166)

## Summary

Fixes `TimeoutNegativeWarning` raised by Node.js v24+ when `setTimeout`
receives a negative timeout value.

#38163

## Root Cause

In `sdk/eventhub/event-hubs/src/withAuth.ts`, a deadline pattern
computes remaining time as `timeoutAfterStartTime - Date.now()`. If
prior steps (opening CBS session, acquiring lock) consume more time than
the original timeout budget, this goes **negative**. The negative value
flows into `setTimeout()` in `requestResponseLink.sendRequest()` and
`lock.acquire()`, triggering:

```
TimeoutNegativeWarning: -1 is a negative number.
Timeout duration was set to 1.
```

This is cosmetic — Node clamps to 1ms and the operation succeeds — but
the warning triggers ops alerts.

The same pattern exists in Service Bus's `linkEntity.ts`.

## Fix

Clamp timeout values to `Math.max(..., 0)` at all affected sites
(defense in depth):

| File | Sites | Change |
|------|-------|--------|
| `sdk/eventhub/event-hubs/src/withAuth.ts` | 4 | Clamp
`timeoutAfterStartTime - Date.now()` |
| `sdk/servicebus/service-bus/src/core/linkEntity.ts` | 3 | Clamp
`timeoutInMs - (Date.now() - startTime)` |
| `sdk/core/core-amqp/src/requestResponseLink.ts` | 1 | Clamp
`timeoutInMs` in `sendRequest()` |
| `sdk/core/core-amqp/src/util/lock.ts` | 1 | Clamp `timeoutInMs` in
`acquire()`'s `setTimeout` |

Co-authored-by: Byron Jin <byronjin@microsoft.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-22T01:06:09Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-22T01:06:09Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Handle AggregateError in core-amqp translate() for Node.js 20+ Happy Eyeballs (#38233)

### Packages impacted by this PR

- `@azure/core-amqp`
- `@azure/event-hubs` (tests only)

### Issues associated with this PR

- Fixes #38231
- Supersedes #38190

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Node.js 20+ defaults to Happy Eyeballs (RFC 8305), racing IPv4/IPv6
connections. DNS failures now throw `AggregateError` instead of a single
system error. `translate()` in core-amqp didn't handle this, returning
raw untranslated errors — breaking `instanceof MessagingError` checks,
`.retryable` logic, and error code handling for all consumers.

### Fix

`translate()` now maps `AggregateError` → new `AggregateError` with all
inner errors translated to `MessagingError`, with `.retryable` stamped
on the aggregate (true if any inner is retryable).

### Are there test cases added in this PR?

Yes — 6 new tests in `core-amqp` covering ENOTFOUND, EAI_AGAIN, mixed
retryability, nested aggregates, and empty aggregates. Event Hubs test
helper updated to unwrap `AggregateError`.

## Package
@azure/cosmos

## Summary

Fixed incorrect SQL filter generation in ORDER BY queries with
continuation tokens. Backslashes and single quotes in `orderByItem`
values are now properly escaped in `formatValueForSQL` before being
embedded in WHERE clauses.

## Root Cause

`formatValueForSQL` embeds continuation token values directly into SQL
WHERE clauses without escaping. Backslash sequences like `\u2013`, `\n`,
`\t` are interpreted by the Cosmos DB SQL parser as unicode escapes or
control characters instead of literal text, causing filter mismatches
that silently skip rows.

## Fix

In `formatValueForSQL`, escape values before embedding in SQL string
literals:

1. **Backslashes:** `\` → `\\` — prevents parser from interpreting
`\uXXXX`, `\n`, `\t` etc. as escape sequences
2. **Single quotes:** `'` → `\u0027` (unicode escape) — prevents string
delimiter confusion

### Why `\u0027` instead of `''` for quotes?

Traditional `''` doubling is ambiguous after `\\`. For value `it\'s`,
after escaping: `'it\\''s'` — parser sees `\\` as literal backslash,
then `'` as end of string (not a `''` pair). `\u0027` is unambiguous in
all contexts.

Backslashes are escaped first, then quotes, so literal `\u0027` in data
becomes `\\u0027` — distinguishable from our injected `\u0027`.

## Testing

Unit tests added covering backslash escaping, quote escaping,
backslash-quote interaction, unicode sequences, and edge cases.

## Summary

Remove dead code, unnecessary type casts, and redundant `async` keywords
across core packages.

### Dead code removal

- `AsyncLockOptions` interface, `Timeout` class (core-amqp)
- `isBearerToken`, `isPopToken` helpers (core-auth)
- `getCachedDefaultHttpsClient`, unused `HttpClient` import
(core-client-rest)
- Stale `declare global { interface Event {} }` (abort-controller)

### Cast removal / type narrowing

- `as any` → `as ParsedOutput` in `parseConnectionString` (core-amqp)
- `{ [k: string]: string }` → `Record` (core-amqp)
- `as string` → `?? ""` for CAE challenge header (core-rest-pipeline)
- `isKeyCredential` param `any` → `unknown` with proper guard
(core-client-rest)
- `objectHasProperty` tightened with null check (core-util)

### Redundant async removal

- `sendRequest`, `sendPolicy` handlers that just return a promise
without awaiting (core-client, core-client-rest, core-http-compat,
core-lro)

### Simplify defensive logic

- **ndJsonPolicy**: Remove unreachable `Array.isArray` guard after
`JSON.parse` on a string starting with `[` (JSON spec guarantees array
result)
- **tokenCycler**: Replace optional chaining with explicit null guard in
`shouldRefresh`, making intent clearer

### Other improvements

- `Resolved` simplified to `Awaited` with deprecation notice
(core-tracing)
- `HttpHeaders` class made non-exported (core-http-compat, was already
not in public API)
- `Set` for passthrough props lookup (core-http-compat)
- XML root key extraction simplified (core-xml)
- URL scope construction fix (core-client)
- Optional chaining removed on guaranteed field (core-paging)

### Packages impacted by this PR

`@azure/ai-projects`

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Release candidate refresh for `@azure/ai-projects` v2.0.2 RC1, updating
the TypeSpec input commit and regenerating client surface area (new
operations + helpers), plus wiring CI for the `sdk/ai` service
directory.

Also fixes a bug in `createFilePartDescriptor`
(`src/static-helpers/multipartHelpers.ts`) where a truthy check `if
(fileInput.contents)` would incorrectly handle cases where `contents` is
an empty string `""`, causing the entire wrapper object to be serialized
as the multipart body instead of just the `contents` value.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

- **Truthy check (previous)**: `if (fileInput.contents)` — fails for
falsy values like empty string `""`.
- **Existence/type guard (chosen)**: `typeof fileInput === "object" &&
fileInput !== null && "contents" in fileInput` — correctly identifies
the wrapper object shape regardless of whether `contents` is falsy.

The existence/type guard approach was chosen because it accurately
distinguishes between the two input shapes (`FileContents` vs. wrapper
object) without relying on the truthiness of the `contents` value.

Additionally, the parameter and return types were tightened from `any`
to well-defined types (`FileContents | { contents: FileContents;
contentType?: string; filename?: string }` and a new
`FilePartDescriptor` interface respectively) for improved type safety.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No new tests added for the multipart helper fix. The change is a
targeted correctness fix to the type guard logic; the existing multipart
serialization is covered by the broader code-based agent operation
tests.
